pairs.question: Which team won the 2016 Stanley Cup and who did they defeat in the finals?
pairs.answer: The Pittsburgh Penguins won the 2016 Stanley Cup by defeating the San Jose Sharks in the finals four games to two.

pairs.question: How many Canadian-based NHL teams qualified for the 2016 Stanley Cup playoffs, and when was the last time this happened?
pairs.answer: None of the NHL's seven Canadian-based teams qualified for the 2016 playoffs. The last time this occurred was in 1970.

pairs.question: What was the Washington Capitals' regular season performance in 2015–16, and why was it significant?
pairs.answer: The Washington Capitals were the Presidents' Trophy winners in the 2015–16 season, meaning they had the most points (best record) during the regular season. This was significant because it marked their first Presidents' Trophy win in franchise history.

pairs.question: How many consecutive playoff appearances did the Detroit Red Wings make before their streak ended in 2016, and where does this rank in NHL history?
pairs.answer: The Detroit Red Wings made 25 consecutive playoff appearances before their streak ended in 2016. This remains tied for the third-longest streak in NHL history.

pairs.question: List the four former WHA teams that missed the 2016 playoffs, and how many times has this occurred since they joined the NHL?
pairs.answer: The four former WHA teams that missed the 2016 playoffs were the Edmonton Oilers, Arizona Coyotes, Carolina Hurricanes, and Colorado Avalanche. This was the fifth time since they joined the league in 1979 that all four missed the playoffs.

pairs.question: How many times have all three California-based NHL teams made the playoffs in the same season, and in which recent years did this occur?
pairs.answer: All three California-based teams (Anaheim Ducks, Los Angeles Kings, and San Jose Sharks) made the playoffs in the same season four times in the past six years, including 2016. The other recent instances were in 2014, 2015, and 2011.

pairs.question: What notable post-season win drought did the New York Islanders end in 2016, and how long was this drought?
pairs.answer: The New York Islanders won their first post-season series since the 1993 Stanley Cup playoffs, ending a 23-year drought, which was the third-longest post-season winless streak in NHL history.
